narrative: All three watchlist coins bounced Saturday, reversing Friday's selloff — solana led at +4.56% while bitcoin and ethereum gained more modestly, even as macro headwinds persist: bitcoin has traded below mining cost for five months with ~20% of miners unprofitable and traders loading bearish options to $52,000 per CoinDesk. Regulatory complexity deepened: CME filed suit plans against the CFTC over Kalshi's perpetual futures approval per CoinDesk, U.S. agencies proposed bank-style KYC rules for stablecoins under the GENIUS Act per CoinDesk, and Illinois added a surprise 0.2% digital asset tax to its state budget per CoinDesk. AI-crypto convergence kept accelerating — Alchemy's AgentCard gained Visa network access, Moody's embedded credit ratings onto Solana, and a Y Combinator startup auto-settles businesses in USDC per CoinDesk.
worth_watching: AI agent payment rails — Alchemy's AgentCard gained Visa network access per CoinDesk, and Y Combinator unveiled an AI that builds, runs, and settles businesses in USDC; the AI-agent-as-payment-entity model is scaling fast and not captured in watchlist coins
Bitcoin L2 shakeout — Botanix shut down per CoinDesk, raising whether the market wants programmable BTC or just yield/lending products; other Bitcoin L2s face the same bear-market viability test

Prediction market turf war — CME plans to sue the CFTC over Kalshi's perpetual futures approval per CoinDesk while Kentucky moved to regulate prediction markets; the outcome shapes whether U.S. prediction market infrastructure fragments or scales

headlines: CME chief executive says company plans to sue CFTC after perpetual futures approval
The U.S. government is betting $2 Billion on quantum computing, and the defense side can't keep up
Stablecoins Were Meant to Disrupt Finance. Instead, They Became Idle Cash.

Crypto's security nightmare won't be solved by ordinary audits
MTSR news: Strategy's STRC preferred stock drops to a record low $89
Live: Bitcoin and ether ETFs lost $111 million combined as rate-cut hopes died
